PM-01.title: Aliasing versus copying
guidance: Draw the object graph before running the append.
Compare aliasing, shallow copying, and deep copying at both nesting levels.
PM-01.code:
from copy import deepcopy
original = {"limits": [10, 20]}
alias = original
shallow = original.copy()
deep = deepcopy(original)
original["limits"].append(30)
print(alias["limits"], shallow["limits"], deep["limits"])
print(alias is original, shallow["limits"] is original["limits"])

PM-02.title: Evaluation order and pattern matching
guidance: Predict which function calls happen before executing the cell.
Change the left operand's truth value, then add malformed command input.
PM-02.code:
events = []
def record(name, value):
    events.append(name); return value
result = record("left", 0) and record("right", 1)
command = {"type": "move", "x": 3, "y": 4}
match command:
    case {"type": "move", "x": x, "y": y}: distance = abs(x) + abs(y)
    case _: distance = None
print(result, events, distance)

PM-03.title: Safe defaults and preserved signatures
guidance: Explain when each default expression is evaluated.
Remove wraps and inspect how the decorated function's metadata changes.

PM-04.title: Descriptor-driven validation
guidance: Trace attribute assignment through __set__ before running it.
Access the descriptor through both the class and an instance.

PM-05.title: Lazy iteration and cleanup
guidance: Predict when the finally block runs for partial and full consumption.
Inject an input failure and verify cleanup still occurs.

PM-06.title: Rollback with ExitStack
guidance: Predict acquisition and release order.
Raise during the second acquisition and inspect partial rollback.
PM-06.code:
from contextlib import ExitStack, contextmanager
@contextmanager
def resource(name):
    print("open", name)
    try: yield name
    finally: print("close", name)
with ExitStack() as stack:
    handles = [stack.enter_context(resource(n)) for n in ("a", "b")]
    print("using", handles)

PM-07.title: Sequence operation costs
guidance: State the expected complexity before measuring.
Vary input size by powers of ten and explain the curve rather than one number.
PM-07.code:
from timeit import timeit
size = 20_000
list_time = timeit("v.insert(0,-1); v.pop(0)", setup=f"v=list(range({size}))", number=500)
deque_time = timeit("v.appendleft(-1); v.popleft()", setup=f"from collections import deque; v=deque(range({size}))", number=500)
print(f"list={list_time:.4f}s deque={deque_time:.4f}s")

PM-08.title: Hash and equality contracts
guidance: Identify the invariant connecting __eq__ and __hash__.
Mutate a key after insertion and explain the failed lookup.

PM-09.title: Set algebra for authorization
guidance: Express the policy as a relation before writing control flow.
Decide whether order and duplicates are requirements before converting to a set.
PM-09.code:
requested = ["read", "write", "admin"]
granted = {"read", "write"}
missing = set(requested) - granted
print("allowed:", not missing, "missing:", missing)
seen = set()
unique = [item for item in requested if not (item in seen or seen.add(item))]
print(unique)

PM-10.title: Text boundaries and buffer views
guidance: Label where bytes become text and which encoding contract applies.
Mutate the backing bytearray and observe the memoryview without copying.
PM-10.code:
packet = bytearray(b"HEADpayload")
view = memoryview(packet)
header, payload = bytes(view[:4]), view[4:]
print(header.decode("ascii"), bytes(payload))
packet[4] = ord("P")
print(bytes(payload))
payload.release(); view.release()

PM-11.title: An immutable value object
guidance: Separate shallow frozen behavior from deep immutability.
Add exact arithmetic and reject operations across currencies.

PM-12.title: Stable heap scheduling
guidance: Write down the tuple comparison order used by heapq.
Remove the sequence number and test jobs that cannot be compared.

PM-13.title: Import caching
guidance: Predict whether module top-level code executes twice.
Inspect __spec__ and sys.modules before constructing a circular-import experiment.
PM-13.code:
import importlib, sys
first = importlib.import_module("json")
second = importlib.import_module("json")
print(first is second, "json" in sys.modules)
print(first.__package__, first.__spec__.origin)

PM-14.title: Structural typing
guidance: Distinguish static Protocol checks from runtime validation.
Remove close and compare type-checker feedback with runtime behavior.

PM-15.title: An API that can evolve
guidance: Identify ownership, mutation, I/O, and error contracts at the boundary.
Replace the boolean flag with a policy when call-site meaning becomes ambiguous.
PM-15.code:
from collections.abc import Iterable
def fetch_users(ids: Iterable[int], *, include_inactive: bool = False) -> list[dict]:
    rows = [{"id": value, "active": value % 2 == 1} for value in ids]
    return rows if include_inactive else [row for row in rows if row["active"]]
print(fetch_users([1, 2, 3]))

PM-16.title: Deterministic time tests
guidance: Inject time instead of sleeping in tests.
Test every attempt boundary and verify the final exception is preserved.

PM-17.title: Bounded input validation
guidance: Validate size before parsing and shape before domain use.
Try missing, extra, nested, and wrong-type fields.

PM-18.title: Inspecting an environment
guidance: Separate interpreter, installation scheme, and distribution metadata.
Repeat inside a clean virtual environment and compare paths.

PM-19.title: Protecting a compound invariant
guidance: Name the state invariant rather than relying on an operation that looks atomic.
Run many threads with and without the lock, then reason beyond one observed result.

PM-20.title: Bounding submitted work
guidance: Separate worker count from total in-flight capacity.
Inject submission and task failures and verify permits are always released.
PM-20.code:
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
from threading import Semaphore
slots, pool = Semaphore(4), ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2)
def submit(function, *args):
    slots.acquire()
    try: future = pool.submit(function, *args)
    except BaseException: slots.release(); raise
    future.add_done_callback(lambda _: slots.release())
    return future
print(submit(sum, [1, 2, 3]).result())
pool.shutdown()

PM-21.title: Process serialization cost
guidance: Measure transfer cost before assuming processes will speed up CPU work.
Choose chunk sizes that balance IPC amortization and load distribution.
PM-21.code:
import multiprocessing as mp, pickle
from time import perf_counter
payload = list(range(200_000))
start = perf_counter()
encoded = pickle.dumps(payload, protocol=pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL)
print("start methods:", mp.get_all_start_methods())
print(f"{len(encoded):,} bytes in {perf_counter()-start:.4f}s")

PM-22.title: Structured cancellation
guidance: Mark every suspension point where cancellation can arrive.
Make one child fail and inspect sibling cleanup and the ExceptionGroup.
PM-22.code:
import asyncio, sys
async def worker(name, delay):
    try:
        await asyncio.sleep(delay); return name
    finally: print("cleanup", name)
async def main():
    async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as group:
        tasks = [group.create_task(worker("a", .01)), group.create_task(worker("b", .02))]
    return [task.result() for task in tasks]
if hasattr(asyncio, "TaskGroup"):
    print(asyncio.run(main()))
else:
    print("TaskGroup requires Python 3.11+; current:", sys.version.split()[0])

PM-23.title: A bounded async queue
guidance: Slow the consumer and locate where producer backpressure appears.
Account for every get with task_done, including failure paths.
PM-23.code:
import asyncio
async def demo():
    queue = asyncio.Queue(maxsize=2)
    async def producer():
        for value in range(4): await queue.put(value)
        await queue.put(None)
    async def consumer():
        while (value := await queue.get()) is not None:
            try: print(value)
            finally: queue.task_done()
        queue.task_done()
    await asyncio.gather(producer(), consumer()); await queue.join()
asyncio.run(demo())

PM-24.title: Length-prefixed framing
guidance: Never assume one recv call returns the requested bytes.
Reject oversized declared lengths before allocation.
PM-24.code:
import socket, struct
left, right = socket.socketpair()
try:
    payload = b"hello"
    left.sendall(struct.pack("!I", len(payload)) + payload)
    header = right.recv(4); size = struct.unpack("!I", header)[0]
    body = bytearray()
    while len(body) < size: body.extend(right.recv(size - len(body)))
    print(bytes(body))
finally: left.close(); right.close()

PM-25.title: A bounded local HTTP exchange
guidance: Separate connect, header, body, and total deadline concerns.
Limit response bytes and decide which operations are safe to retry.
PM-25.code:
from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer
from threading import Thread
from urllib.request import urlopen
class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    def do_GET(self):
        body=b"ok"; self.send_response(200); self.send_header("Content-Length","2"); self.end_headers(); self.wfile.write(body)
    def log_message(self,*_): pass
server=ThreadingHTTPServer(("127.0.0.1",0),Handler); Thread(target=server.serve_forever,daemon=True).start()
try:
    with urlopen(f"http://127.0.0.1:{server.server_port}/",timeout=1) as response: print(response.status,response.read(16))
finally: server.shutdown(); server.server_close()

PM-26.title: Async admission control
guidance: Decide whether admission happens before or after creating a task.
Design shutdown to stop acceptance and drain bounded in-flight work.
PM-26.code:
import asyncio
async def handle(reader, writer, slots):
    async with slots:
        data = await reader.read(100); writer.write(data.upper()); await writer.drain()
    writer.close(); await writer.wait_closed()
async def main():
    slots=asyncio.Semaphore(10)
    server=await asyncio.start_server(lambda r,w: handle(r,w,slots),"127.0.0.1",0)
    print(server.sockets[0].getsockname()); server.close(); await server.wait_closed()
asyncio.run(main())

PM-27.title: Secure TLS defaults
guidance: Name the guarantees lost when verification is disabled.
Keep authentication, authorization, transport encryption, and secret handling separate.
PM-27.code:
import hmac, ssl
context = ssl.create_default_context()
print("hostname checks:", context.check_hostname)
print("verification mode:", context.verify_mode)
print("signature matches:", hmac.compare_digest(b"signed", b"signed"))

PM-28.title: CPU and allocation evidence
guidance: Write a performance hypothesis before selecting a profiler.
Repeat measurements and distinguish CPU, wall time, allocation, I/O, and queueing.
PM-28.code:
import cProfile, tracemalloc
work = lambda: sum(value * value for value in range(50_000))
tracemalloc.start(); profiler = cProfile.Profile(); profiler.enable()
result = work(); profiler.disable()
current, peak = tracemalloc.get_traced_memory(); tracemalloc.stop()
print(result, "peak bytes:", peak)
profiler.print_stats(sort="cumtime")

PM-29.title: Complexity before constants
guidance: Include index construction for one-shot workloads.
Measure memory and find the query count where precomputation pays off.
PM-29.code:
from timeit import timeit
users=[{"id":v} for v in range(10_000)]; queries=list(range(0,10_000,10))
def scan(): return [next(u for u in users if u["id"]==q) for q in queries]
index={u["id"]:u for u in users}
def indexed(): return [index[q] for q in queries]
print("scan",timeit(scan,number=3)); print("index",timeit(indexed,number=3))

PM-30.title: A measurable bounded handler
guidance: Establish correctness and malformed-input tests before load testing.
Measure serialization, queueing, and compute separately before optimizing.
